title: Enterosora subpinnata
taxonomy: Enterosora subpinnata is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that belongs to the genus Enterosora. Enterosora subpinnata is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Enterosora subpinnata is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. This fern is known for its unique sub-pinnate leaves and is often found growing on trees and rocks in its natural habitat.

intro: Enterosora subpinnata is a tropical epiphytic fern native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique pinnate leaves and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in treating various conditions including wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Enterosora subpinnata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Enterosora subpinnata is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, often used to treat digestive issues, and has also been used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
